lead.title: Lebanese Hold Fast to Their Land Despite Threat of Long Israeli Occupation
lead.snippet: Entire towns in southern Lebanon have been hollowed out by the war between Israel and Hezbollah. Some residents have stayed, fearing permanent displacement.

items.title: Live Updates: U.S. Forces Begin New Round of Strikes on Iran

items.title: U.K. to Change Law to Allow Deportation of ‘Grooming Gang’ Leader
items.snippet: The British government on Monday laid out steps enabling the deportation to Pakistan of Shabir Ahmed, the leader of a gang that raped dozens of girls in Northern England.

items.snippet: Facing threats from Russia and China, Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is turning to help from Western allies in forming a centralized agency.

items.title: The Next Phase in Ukraine’s War With Russia: The Battle for Minds

items.snippet: They began as supply mules. Now ground robots evacuate the wounded, hold trenches and even do the killing.

items.snippet: Many say communication was chaotic and even nonexistent as flames tore through the area, taking 12 lives in one of the deadliest wildfires on record in Spain.

items.title: The Netherlands Pushes to Make Manure a Hotter Product
items.snippet: The Netherlands has been trying to figure out what to do with manure it can no longer use thanks to environmental rules. Volatile fertilizer costs may help.

items.title: Hard-Liners in Iran Want to Keep Fighting America

items.snippet: President Volodymyr Zelensky said Ukraine’s prime minister, Yuliia Svyrydenko, would step down amid a broader shake-up in Ukraine’s senior leadership.

items.title: Lindsey Graham Championed Israel, Even as Wars Eroded American Support

items.title: Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa, Ruler Who Transformed Qatar, Dies at 74
items.snippet: Under Sheikh Hamad, the tiny nation of Qatar developed immense natural gas wealth and cultivated influence far beyond its borders.

lead.title: Uber’s product chief on hotels, robotaxis, and why the company doesn’t want to be “everything for everyone”
